title: Add configurable chat response length limit with multi-message splitting
description:
## Problem
The bot's in-game chat responses have no configurable length limit. Minecraft's chat has a ~256 character limit, but the agent frequently produces longer responses (e.g. inventory listings + explanations). Currently, responses over the limit get silently truncated by Minecraft with no feedback.

Additionally, there's no mechanism to auto-split long responses across multiple chat messages to preserve the full response.

## Desired Outcome
- Configurable `ChatMaxResponseLength` in `ChatOptions` (default 500 characters)
- Responses longer than `ChatMaxResponseLength` are split into multiple Chat actions, each at most `ChatMaxResponseLength` chars
- The split preserves whole sentences where possible (split on ". " or ".  " or sentence boundary)
- Logging indicates when splitting occurs

## Implementation Plan
1. Add `ChatMaxResponseLength` to `ChatOptions` (default 500)
2. Add a helper method to split long strings into chunks at sentence boundaries
3. In `AgentBackgroundService.ProcessChatResultAsync`, use the helper to split `pendingResponse` into multiple Chat actions instead of one
4. Update `appsettings.json` example and doc comments in `ChatOptions.cs`
5. Update `appsettings.Development.json` if needed
6. Update `ChatOptions` XML doc example in `ChatOptions.cs`

## Files
- `Agent.Planning/Llm/ChatOptions.cs` — add `ChatMaxResponseLength` option
- `WebUI.Blazor/AgentBackgroundService.cs` — use splitter when dispatching chat responses
- `WebUI.Blazor/appsettings.json` — document new option

## Priority
Medium
